item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.6 No.18-21, 1958
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.handwritten_text:
Withdrawal
Felix Topolski
Denise Carter & Viart [?]
sketch_descriptions: A large pen sketch showing a scene with multiple figures, including a man lying on the ground in the foreground and groups of people standing around, some holding sticks or weapons.
A line of loosely drawn figures in various poses and garb, suggesting a market or street scene with people walking and interacting.
A seated woman at a table, possibly engaged in writing or reading, with other standing figures nearby.
A charcoal or heavily shaded drawing of three people standing close together, two facing forward and one wearing a hat, appearing to be in conversation or a group interaction.
A detailed sketch of three people seated around a table with drinks, one holding a fan and another with a raised fist, indicating a casual social setting.
A darker sketch showing two older individuals seated at a table, one playing a wind instrument, with faint figures in the background, evoking a scene of music or gathering.
pages.page_summary: This page features a variety of expressive sketches by Felix Topolski, depicting social scenes including groups of people in interaction, a market-like environment, and intimate gatherings around tables.
